rationale.R1.2: A general request to look at a research paragraph centrally includes checking whether the stated premises, theoretical claims, and conclusions cohere.
rationale.R1.3: The paragraph makes performance and generalization claims tied to reported experimental results, so claim-evidence alignment is a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.6: Because the delegation targets a paragraph rather than a full paper, sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, register, and concision are centrally implicated.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of such an open-ended request should preserve uncertainty about what kind of review was intended.
rationale.RX.2: The paragraph contains broad technical claims, making avoidance of overclaiming an always-active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities and not actually review or rewrite the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: Potential downstream technical misuse from unsupported scientific claims makes harm avoidance active.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should remain traceable to the delegation wording and the artifact’s stated claims.
